cases.title: Testing-plan §6.1 E2E-01 and §6.2 desktop browser matrix cover NFR-15 projection QR scenarios
preconditions: Testing-plan §6.1 E2E-01 (lecturer opens session and displays rotating QR) and §6.2 Chromium desktop lecturer flow apply.
NFR-15 requires legible classroom projection display with clear refresh indication and open/closed state feedback per 07-non-functional-risk §2.5.
Fixture matrix seeds Open session with rotating QR, Closed session, and lecturer assignment per attendance-open-session fixture.
steps: Map NFR-15 projection requirements to testing-plan §3 Session lifecycle and QR row (AC-01, AC-02, AC-05) and E2E-01 journey.
Verify integration harness can drive session open, qr/current rotation metadata, and session close state transitions.
Confirm desktop Chromium (lecturer/admin flow) is the default harness context for PG-05 projection scenarios per testing-plan §6.2.
Record pass/fail per scenario against NFR-15 legibility, refresh indication, and state feedback requirements.
cases.expected: E2E-01 and testing-plan Session lifecycle and QR row fully represent NFR-15 projection scenarios: Open session enables rotating QR display, refresh indication is verifiable via expiresAt/countdown metadata, and Closed state suppresses active QR per 02-business-workflow §3.2 step 6.

cases.title: M04 QR orchestrator returns projection-ready display bundle with session identity and TTL metadata at DB boundary
preconditions: Integration harness uses real DB transaction boundaries for qr_session_tokens and class_sessions tables.
An Open class session row exists with known sessionId, section code, session name, and room.
M04 getCurrentToken(sessionId) and session read path are callable through service layer.
steps: Invoke M04 getCurrentToken(sessionId) for the Open session.
Query qr_session_tokens and assert Valid row bound to sessionId with qrPayload resolvable server-side.
Join class_sessions and class_sections to retrieve section code and session display name.
Assert returned display bundle includes classSessionId, tokenState Valid, expiresAt within 30-second window, qrPayload, session state Open, section code, and session name.
Advance harness clock beyond TTL and re-invoke getCurrentToken; assert refreshed qrPayload with new expiresAt for projection countdown synchronization.
cases.expected: M04 module + repository integration returns projection-ready payload per 07-domain-specific-components §2.1: all fields required by QrDisplayPanel (DC-01) and QrCountdownRing (DC-02) are coherently bound for classroom projection without trusting client-supplied session hints.

cases.title: QrDisplayPanel data binding follows session state: Open enables projection QR, Closed and Cancelled suppress Valid token
preconditions: Session fixtures exist in Scheduled, Open, Closed, and Cancelled states.
M03 session control and M04 QR orchestrator share the same database.
Display read path queries session state before returning QR metadata per 12-ui-states §5.1.
steps: Attempt qr/current read for Scheduled session and record absence of Valid token or appropriate guard response.
Execute open transition (Scheduled to Open) and verify Valid token with expiresAt is returned.
Record beforeState Open with active qrPayload; invoke close transition (Open to Closed).
Query qr/current for Closed session and assert no Valid token with active qrPayload.
Attempt qr/current on Cancelled session fixture and assert QR display data is suppressed.
cases.expected: beforeState Scheduled blocks Valid QR display data; Open enables projection-ready Valid token with state feedback metadata; after Open to Closed or Cancelled, display binding suppresses active QR per QrDisplayPanel states spec and NFR-15 closed-state feedback requirement.

cases.title: GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/qr/current returns 200 projection-ready envelope with countdown synchronization fields
preconditions: Assigned Lecturer is authenticated with Authorization bearer token.
Target session is currently Open with known section code and session name.
E2E harness validates standard { data, meta, error } response envelope per API §2.3.
steps: Send GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/qr/current with Authorization header.
Validate HTTP 200 and JSON envelope shape { data, meta, error } with error null.
Assert data contains classSessionId matching path param, tokenState Valid, expiresAt ISO-8601 UTC, and non-empty qrPayload suitable for high-contrast QR canvas rendering.
Assert meta includes requestId and timestamp for QrCountdownRing synchronization.
Re-call endpoint after one TTL rollover and confirm HTTP 200 with refreshed expiresAt and updated qrPayload.
cases.expected: Endpoint returns HTTP 200 with stable envelope schema per API design §4.4; qr/current data exposes all fields required to render large high-contrast QR with countdown refresh indicator on lecturer projection view per NFR-15.

cases.title: WF-02 primary flow: open session enables projection QR display with session identity, rotation, and Open state feedback
preconditions: Lecturer account and Scheduled session fixture are available per 02-business-workflow §3.2.
Session has section code, session name, room, and no prior openedAt timestamp.
FLOW-05 / E2E-01 lecturer session operations journey applies.
steps: Call POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/open as assigned Lecturer.
Assert response data.state Open with qr preview containing expiresAt and qrPayload.
Call GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/qr/current and verify tokenState Valid bound to the same sessionId.
Call GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance and assert data.state is Open with live count summary for PG-05 status strip.
Wait one TTL cycle and confirm qr/current returns refreshed token without session reopen or lecturer page reload.
cases.expected: WF-02 §3.2 happy path: session open commits Open state and projection display receives first Valid QR with session name, section code, countdown metadata, Open status feedback, and auto-rotation every ~30 seconds without lecturer reload per NFR-15.

cases.title: WF-02 alternate flow: session close transitions projection display from live QR to locked Closed state feedback
preconditions: Open session with active QR rotation is displayed on lecturer view.
Assigned Lecturer is authenticated.
Enrolled students exist with mixed attendance states.
steps: Confirm GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/qr/current returns tokenState Valid.
Call POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/close as assigned Lecturer.
Assert response data.state Closed with closedAt timestamp.
Call GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/qr/current and record whether tokenState is non-Valid or endpoint returns appropriate error.
Call GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance and assert data.state is Closed.
cases.expected: After Open to Closed transition, projection display must not show active rotating QR; Closed state message and Closed status badge replace QR panel per QrDisplayPanel states spec and NFR-15 open/closed state feedback requirement.

cases.title: Student, ITAdmin, and out-of-scope Lecturer are denied QR projection display endpoint
preconditions: Student, ITAdmin, and Lecturer-not-assigned-to-section test accounts exist with valid tokens per 01-roles-permissions.md §3.1.
An Open session exists in a section the out-of-scope Lecturer is not assigned to.
Assigned Lecturer token is available as positive control.
steps: Call GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/qr/current as Student.
Call GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/qr/current as ITAdmin.
Call GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/qr/current as out-of-scope Lecturer.
Read HTTP status and error.code for each denied request (expect 403 Forbidden or OutOfScope).
Call GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/qr/current as assigned Lecturer and assert HTTP 200 with Valid token metadata.
Verify no qrPayload leakage in denied response bodies.
cases.expected: Unauthorized roles cannot retrieve QR projection metadata per SessionControl.execute deny rules; assigned Lecturer may fetch current QR; no qrPayload leakage to denied actors per NFR-09 and BR-19.

cases.title: QR display endpoint rejects or omits Valid token when session is not Open
preconditions: Assigned Lecturer is authenticated.
Session fixtures exist in Scheduled and Closed states.
steps: Call GET /v1/class-sessions/{scheduledSessionId}/qr/current for a Scheduled session.
Record HTTP status and whether data.tokenState is absent or non-Valid.
Open a separate session, then close it; call GET /v1/class-sessions/{closedSessionId}/qr/current.
Assert response does not return tokenState Valid with active qrPayload for Closed session.
Verify projection UI would render locked/hidden state rather than scannable QR for non-Open sessions.
cases.expected: NFR-15 restricts active projection QR to Open sessions only: Scheduled and Closed targets do not expose active Valid rotating QR metadata; display shows appropriate non-scannable locked state with correct Closed/Scheduled feedback.

cases.title: Concurrent qr/current fetches during display rotation return consistent projection metadata
preconditions: Open session exists with QR rotation scheduler active.
Integration harness can dispatch parallel GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/qr/current requests.
Assigned Lecturer auth context is established.
steps: Fire multiple parallel GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/qr/current requests at a fixed instant before TTL boundary.
Collect all responses and assert each returns HTTP 200 with tokenState Valid for the same sessionId.
Assert all responses share the same qrPayload and expiresAt for the current rotation window.
Repeat parallel fetches immediately after TTL rollover and confirm all responses converge on the next valid token without corrupt display state.
cases.expected: Concurrent reads during a rotation window return consistent current token metadata for projection display; post-TTL parallel reads converge on the next Valid token so QrDisplayPanel refresh indication remains coherent under class-start load.

cases.title: PG-05 QrDisplayPanel renders large high-contrast QR with section code, session name, countdown, and Open badge for projection
preconditions: Lecturer is logged in on desktop Chromium per testing-plan §6.2.
An Open session is available on PG-05 session control page.
QrDisplayPanel (DC-01) and QrCountdownRing (DC-02) components are mounted.
steps: Navigate to /lecturer/sessions/{sessionId} (PG-05) and observe QrDisplayPanel.
Verify section code and session name appear in panel header alongside Open StatusBadge.
Assert QR canvas is high-contrast black-on-white without radius, gradients, or shadow on QR modules per DC-01 spec.
Verify QR occupies substantial viewport area (≥ 60% height in projection mode) and QrCountdownRing shows numeric seconds-remaining.
Confirm SessionControlBar shows room and scheduled time context per wireframe PG-05.
cases.expected: Browser journey satisfies AC-UI-06 and NFR-15 projection legibility: large high-contrast QR suitable for classroom projector with session identity, Open status badge, and countdown refresh indicator visible on one screen.

cases.title: QrDisplayPanel auto-refreshes QR with clear QrCountdownRing indication before TTL expiry without manual page reload
preconditions: Lecturer is logged in on desktop Chromium.
An Open session is displayed on PG-05 with QrDisplayPanel and QrCountdownRing visible.
Session name, section code, and Open status badge are rendered.
steps: Navigate to /lecturer/sessions/{sessionId} (PG-05) and observe QrDisplayPanel with high-contrast QR image and countdown indicator.
Note the visible countdown value and QR image fingerprint or payload preview.
Wait past one 30-second TTL cycle without refreshing the browser tab.
Observe QrCountdownRing reset and QR image crossfade or swap to a new token before prior expiry.
Verify session badge remains Open, session name and section code stay visible, and no full-page reload occurred.
Confirm refreshed QR remains scannable for student check-in flow.
cases.expected: Browser journey demonstrates NFR-15 clear refresh indication: QR refreshes automatically before 30-second TTL expires via QrDisplayPanel/QrCountdownRing without lecturer manual reload, maintaining Open-state indicators throughout rotation per 07-domain-specific-components §2.1.

cases.title: Closed session PG-05 hides QR canvas and shows locked state message with Closed badge
preconditions: Lecturer is logged in on desktop Chromium.
A Closed session fixture exists with prior attendance data.
Session was previously Open with active QR display.
steps: Navigate to /lecturer/sessions/{closedSessionId} (PG-05).
Verify StatusBadge shows Closed state.
Assert QrDisplayPanel does not render scannable QR canvas; locked state message is visible instead.
Confirm QrCountdownRing is hidden per 12-ui-states §5.1 Closed state row.
Confirm Open/Close attendance CTAs are disabled or hidden per wireframe Closed state.
Verify section code and session name remain visible for classroom context.
cases.expected: PG-05 Closed state per wireframe §4.2: QR locked message replaces projection canvas; Closed badge displayed; no active countdown or rotating QR; session identity remains legible per NFR-15 open/closed state feedback.

cases.title: QrCountdownRing warning styling in final 5 seconds signals imminent QR rotation to lecturer
preconditions: Lecturer is logged in on desktop Chromium at PG-05 with Open session.
QrCountdownRing (DC-02) is active with 30-second TTL cycle.
07-domain-specific-components §2.2 specifies warning token family in final ~5 seconds.
steps: Navigate to /lecturer/sessions/{sessionId} (PG-05) immediately after QR token issuance.
Observe QrCountdownRing at > 5 seconds remaining and record default styling.
Wait until countdown reaches ≤ 5 seconds remaining.
Assert ring transitions to warning token family styling per DC-02 spec.
Verify numeric seconds-remaining label remains visible (e.g. aria-live 'Còn 4 giây' per 13-accessibility-basics.md).
Confirm QR refreshes before expiry and countdown resets to full cycle.
cases.expected: QrCountdownRing provides clear refresh indication: warning styling in final ~5 seconds alerts lecturer of imminent rotation; numeric label remains accessible; rotation completes without manual reload per NFR-15.

cases.title: Typical projector viewport 1280x720 preserves QR legibility and state indicators without clipping
preconditions: Lecturer is logged in on desktop Chromium.
Open session with active QR rotation is displayed on PG-05.
Harness can resize browser to 1280x720 (common classroom projector resolution).
steps: Set viewport to 1280x720 and navigate to /lecturer/sessions/{sessionId} (PG-05).
Measure QR canvas dimensions relative to viewport height.
Verify section code, session name, Open StatusBadge, and QrCountdownRing are fully visible without horizontal scroll or clipped text.
Assert QR modules remain high-contrast black-on-white at projector resolution.
Wait one TTL cycle and confirm refresh indication remains visible at 1280x720.
cases.expected: At typical classroom projector resolution (1280x720), NFR-15 projection legibility is maintained: QR occupies substantial viewport area, all state feedback elements remain readable, and refresh indication is visible without layout breakage.

cases.title: Physical classroom projector pilot verifies NFR-15 legibility under real projection conditions
preconditions: Staging pilot has access to classroom projector and lectern display setup.
Open session with rotating QR is available on PG-05.
Physical projector brightness, ambient lighting, and throw distance are not reproducible in Playwright MCP harness.
steps: Lecturer opens PG-05 on lectern browser and projects display to classroom screen.
Students at back of room attempt to scan projected QR during one full TTL cycle.
Verify QR remains scannable and countdown/refresh indication is visible from rear seating.
Close session and confirm Closed state message and badge are legible on projected display.
Document any legibility issues for triage.
cases.expected: Physical classroom projection delivers NFR-15 legibility with clear refresh indication and open/closed state feedback under real ambient lighting; provides production-like evidence beyond Chromium desktop emulation.
